subject: Ecology
title: Mycorrhizal Networks
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Mycorrhizal Networks.

hook.text: Over 90% of all plant species form partnerships with underground fungi to survive.

buildup.text: Mycorrhizal fungi extend hair-thin threads from roots far into the soil to mine nutrients.

discovery.text: The fungi deliver phosphorus and water to the plant. The plant pays in sugar.

twist.text: These networks connect neighboring trees, letting them share resources across the forest.

climax.text: Without mycorrhizae, most forests, grasslands, and crops would collapse.

punchline.text: Roots alone aren't enough. Fungi complete the job.

quiz.question: What do mycorrhizal fungi provide to plants?
options: Phosphorus and water in exchange for sugar
